text:
Matildas captain Sam Kerr has been ruled out of the Paris 2024 Olympics as the football star continues rehab for her knee injury.

Matildas captain Sam Kerr has been ruled out of the upcoming Paris 2024 Olympics five months after injuring her knee.

Tony Gustavsson, the women’s national coach, on Tuesday revealed a 23-player squad for the upcoming friendlies against China in Adelaide and Sydney over the coming two weeks which did not include Kerr or attacker Amy Sayer who are both recovering from ACL injuries.

It was also revealed the Aussie captain will also not feature at the upcoming Olympics, with the upcoming friendlies an opportunity for Mr Gustavsson to finalise the Paris squad.

“I think everyone can do the math here and understand that obviously the Olympic roster will be based mostly out of those that are in this upcoming May/June camp,” Gustavsson said, adding the selection process would be difficult.

"This window will be a tough one for me and my staff in terms of evaluating players, where they are, and then the final selection process for Paris.

“We want to use this camp to summarise the evolved playing style we have built upon in the last couple of months. To go back to what we did at last year’s FIFA Women’s World Cup and the core things we did well there and then add the layers we have worked on since.

“Then we will see if we can get more of a complete overview of where we are positioned, work on nuances and details and get some answers of where are we and what do we need to keep working on come the Olympics.”

Kerr ruptured her ACL while training with Chelsea at the start of the year and is about halfway through her rehabilitation process.

Despite her injury, the 30-year-old footballer has had an eventful year.

In January, Kerr was charged with racially aggravated harassment against a police officer in the United Kingdom over an incident one year prior where she she was accused of calling an officer a “stupid white bastard” over a taxi fare dispute.

The Aussie footballer spent a night behind bars after the incident and pled not guilty to a public disorder charge under the UK’s Crime and Disorder Act.

The footballer will appear in court in February next year to fight the officer’s claims.
